steps.title: Find the true subject
steps.content: The sentence opens "The artistic talents of Barbara Chase-Riboud..." The head noun is talents — plural. The prepositional phrase "of Barbara Chase-Riboud" is not the subject and does not affect agreement. The blank needs a plural verb.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'hasn't been': singular contraction ("has not been"); does not agree with the plural subject talents. ✗
  • Choice B — 'wasn't': singular past tense ("was not"); does not agree with the plural subject talents. ✗
  • Choice C — 'isn't': singular present tense ("is not"); does not agree with the plural subject talents. ✗
  • Choice D — 'aren't': plural present tense ("are not"); agrees with the plural subject talents. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
